source: EDIT-101 NHP data, FDA 2024 Gene Editing Guidance
description: Gene editing-specific expected effects when delivered by AAV. Inherits full AAV gene therapy profile (~31 entries) plus editing-specific additions. Key concern: prolonged Cas9 expression from persistent AAV episomes increases cumulative off-target editing risk.

expected_findings.rationale: Successful gene knockout/correction. E.g., 52-96% TTR reduction (NTLA-2001); >60% HbF (BEAM-101).

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: 58-79% prevalence in humans; universal in NHP
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Titer-dependent; may limit redosing or cause immune-mediated organ injury
expected_findings.rationale: Prior S. aureus/S. pyogenes exposure creates pre-existing anti-Cas9 immunity. Treatment amplifies response.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: T-cell-mediated clearance of edited cells = loss of therapeutic effect
expected_findings.rationale: Memory T cells from prior bacterial exposure. More concerning for AAV delivery due to prolonged Cas9 expression from persistent episomes.

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: ~33% of TP53-WT cell lines show detectable p53 activation
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Selection of p53-null clones = oncogenic concern
expected_findings.rationale: DSB-induced DNA damage response activates p53. Nuclease editors only — base/prime editors use nicks, not DSBs.
NHEJ between multiple DSB sites. Nuclease editors only.

expected_findings.description: Large deletions (>1 kb) at DSB sites
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Deletion of tumor suppressor or essential gene = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Error-prone DSB repair. Nuclease editors only.

expected_findings.description: AAV vector integration at Cas9-induced DSB sites
expected_findings.rationale: AAV episomes can integrate at DSB sites created by Cas9. Unique to AAV-delivered nuclease editing.
